name: Axel Stordahl
abstract: Axel Stordahl (August 8, 1913 – August 30, 1963) was an American arranger and composer who was active from the late 1930s through the 1950s. He is perhaps best known for his work with Frank Sinatra in the 1940s at Columbia Records.

about_quotes.speaker: Charlie Barnet
about_quotes.evidence: The interviewee mentions Axel Stordahl only in the context of the interviewer's question about June Hutton's marriage; the interviewee does not discuss any personal or professional interaction with Stordahl.

about_quotes.speaker: Joe Bushkin
about_quotes.evidence: Sy Oliver and Alex Stordahl with some strips of music, five lines to change a riff here and there, in order to match up all the singers doing it and so forth, and make a production out of it.
about_quotes.doc_title: Joe Bushkin interviewed by Monk Rowe, Los Angeles, California, February 15, 1999

about_quotes.speaker: Steve Buckingham,
about_quotes.evidence: But when he got with Capitol in LA and especially with Nelson Riddle and Axel Stordahl and oh the great trumpet player.
about_quotes.doc_title: Steve Buckingham, interviewed by Monk Rowe, Clinton, New York, February 25, 2019 [interview 1 of 2]

about_quotes.speaker: Skitch Henderson
about_quotes.evidence: I made a record with Sinatra, the first record he made I played piano... Axel Stordahl made the arrangements.
about_quotes.doc_title: Skitch Henderson interviewed by Monk Rowe, New Milford, Connecticut, October 31, 1998

about_quotes.speaker: William Randolph "Cozy" Cole
about_quotes.evidence: [INTERVIEWER] Axel Stordahl. [INTERVIEWER] Axel Stordahl, that's the one, that's the one.
